Regulatory Basis: Weight loss treatment claims imply disease or drug effect under FDA
Suggested Alternative: “Clinically studied ingredients that support metabolic health”

Regulatory Basis: Obesity is a disease state under FDA
Suggested Alternative: Avoid disease reference; use structure/function claims

Regulatory Basis: Strong appetite suppression implies pharmacologic action
Adjustment Rule Applied: Replace absolute control language with supportive wording

Regulatory Basis: Explicit receptor blockade implies pharmacological drug action
Adjustment Rule Applied: Remove receptor-specific mechanistic language from consumer claims
Suggested Alternative: Do not reference receptor; use general appetite support wording

Regulatory Basis: Enzyme activation claims may imply drug mechanism
Adjustment Rule Applied: Soften mechanism and remove fat-loss guarantee
Suggested Alternative: “Supports metabolic pathways involved in energy utilization”

Regulatory Basis: Must avoid measurable or guaranteed metabolic increase claims
Fat reduction claims imply body composition change beyond structure/function
Suggested Alternative: “Supports fat metabolism as part of a healthy lifestyle”

Regulatory Basis: Acceptable if not framed as treatment

Suggested Alternative: “Supports healthy body composition when combined with diet and exercise”
